posts.author: Rits
posts.text: Am I investing or just donating money to the market? — If your investment is more than 3+ years & you have conviction then stay invested irrespective of portfolio returns. IMO retailors lack conviction ,experience ,discipline & patience in market. Red or Green Portfolio never define success / failure. Just ask yourself, if you’ve invested 5 years back in nifty index or any value stocks (Not recommendation) you can realize your portfolio will always be in green with more than 18% + returns. (worst case scenario). I remember famous warren buffett quote " Stock market is tool to transfer money fr…

posts.author: Ranjit_DailyTrader
posts.text: Am I investing or just donating money to the market? — agreed!! “Learning phase” ends when you start tracking results like a business, not vibes from YouTube. If there’s no strategy, no risk management, and no journal… it’s not learning — it’s tuition fees to the market.

posts.author: BB789
posts.text: Am I investing or just donating money to the market? — If you’re trading only a few weeks, put only money what you can afford to lose. It’ll usually take years before you’re profitable. The trick is to not blow your capital until then.

posts.author: Ranjit_DailyTrader
posts.text: Am I investing or just donating money to the market? — Started trading with full confidence, watched a few YouTube videos, and now my portfolio is consistently red. At what point do we accept that we are “learning” vs just losing money?
